entry.title: Dynamic date filter in SAP Data Intelligence Cloud operator
entry.content:
Hello Community,
 
I am using SAP Application consumer Gen2 operator to fetch odata calls from an entity. I am able to filter the data with odata query using below settings.
 
"selectOptions": [
{
"name": "lastModifiedDateTime",
"elements": [
{
"comparison": ">=",
"low": "2023-06-01 00:00:00.1234"
}
]
}
]
 
 
However, I want to run the pipeline with automatic lastModifiedDateTime like Today's date or specific days from today. How can I define this in the diagram/json format of the operator? Is there a way to define custom variables in the pipeline and automatic query to populate the variable without manual intervention?
 
Any suggestions are appreciated.
 
Regards,
Kiran

entry.title: Custom Fields (Characteristics) for Margin Analysis (Profitability Analysis) in S/4HANA Cloud Public
entry.content:
1. Basic Information about custom Characteristics (Custom Fields)
  • Key users can add custom fields to margin analysis by utilizing the "Accounting: Market Segment" business context.
  • You can add up to 120 custom fields using the "Accounting: Market Segment" business context.
  • With this context, you can include the following types of custom fields:
    • Code List (Recommended)
    •  Numerical Text
    • Text

 

  • Custom fields can be deleted even if postings have already been made using them.

 

  1. Custom Field (Characteristic) Creation

Custom Field App:

Clear Create (+)

Type option you have:

  • Code List (Recommended)
  • Numerical Text
  • Text

Click: Create and Edit

You need to activate the fields where they are required. Each section provides available options; identify your area of interest or need and activate the relevant fields accordingly.

We have Enabled below services under User Interfaces:

Cost Centers Actuals

Manage Journal Entries

Post General Journal Entries

Manage Recurring Journal Entries

Journal Entry Item and many other.

Save

and then Publish. It may take a while.

It got published.

 

  1. Inputting Values for Custom Fields

There are three following options to enter values for custom fields:

  • Substitution Rules

You can create substitution rules for custom fields through the "Accounting: Market Segment" business context, available in the Manage Substitution/Validation Rules app.

  • Manual Selection in Manual Entries

Field values for custom fields can be entered manually.

  • Substitution Using Business Add-In (BAdI)

The relevant enhancement option is the Business Add-In Market Segment Extension Fields Substitution (FINS_MARKET_SEGMENT_SUB), which allows you to determine extension characteristics using the market segment.

- Create a custom field through a pre-existing CDS view.

- Generate a custom field by creating a derivation rule through a custom business object.

- Develop a custom field based on another custom field that was previously derived.

(Please Consult with Technical Consultant)

Manage Substitution / Validation Rules – Journal Entry App

Create

 

Substitution Type: Substitute with Constant

Save

Then Activate.

 

  1. Transaction Posting with Manually Custom Fields Update

Create Outgoing Invoice (FI Customer Billing)

Region Field

Note: A functional consultant can manually derive a custom field (characteristic) or through substitution. However, if you wish to use BAdi, you will need to seek assistance from a technical consultant.

entry.title: material requirement based on products declared ?
entry.content:
Hi all,

I wonder how to tackle following issue: 

Every now and then process orders of i.e. 1234567 material production will generate waste that is packed into empty bag (i.e 7654321) 

At the minute each process order for this material (1234567) expects 1 or 2 empty bags (7654321) just in case if it generate waste so I have a lot of POs that expected consumption of empty bags is 1 or 2 but actual consumption none because not all of the process orders will generate waste or rather - not all of them declare it waiting till it reaches certain level worth packing away and disposing. From compliance point of view it looks like we're not issuing expected material into each PO. 

Is it possible to set BOM up so Process Order expects empty bag material to be consumed only when waste material has been declared during production ? Also is it possible to be even more specific i.e. If declared 450kg = 1bag is expected, if above = 2bags etc ? 

Or perhaps there's other way to improve this ? 

Thanks,

JJ

entry.title: Re: Modeler in SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content:
Dear @Sandyk,

Q Can you use the SAP Analytics Cloud 'modeller' to create models based on Datasphere?

A. No. The idea is to completely bypass the need to create models in SAC. Instead, you just directly consume the model you built in Datasphere (DSP) within SAC. This means you don't need to (can't!) maintain a model in SAC when you're consuming a model in DSP.

entry.title: Integrating a Makerspace inventory to create an Immersive Experience using Build Code
entry.content:
This year we are celebrating 10 years of d-shop in Newtown Square. d-shop is SAPs internal maker space, a community collaboration that allows employees and visitors alike to experience new technology, share hobbies and turn ideas into reality.

There are ~39 community run d-shops globally withinSAP all with their own local flavor.

We have been fortunate in Newtown Square to have been able to support various conference and user group build projects. This has the wonderful side effect of leaving around a number of physical assets that we can make available to the local community.

We have Robots, Drones, Microcontrollers, xR headsets amongst our inventory. As a community room, we want the equipment we own to be used, not hidden away in cabinets. To enable this we have been using an Inventory Application that visitors can use to find and reserve items. Our original application was d-shop community built and ran on XSC. A maker space is there to enable innovation, so not only did we use it to track inventory and allow checkin/checkout, we also integrated with the physical room. When an item was checked in and out, Hue lighting would react, Amazon Echo and Google Home were added to provide voice interactivity. We tried NFC, QR codes and mobility to enable simple asset tracking.

 

Time has come to for a renewal, we are starting to see increased engagement in the d-shop, 3d printers are running at capacity, workshop requests are increasing. What a better time to take stock of the room and create a new integrated Inventory Application.

This is a journey, we are open to new ideas and integrations as we grow. Some ideas we have don't have any practical application beyond it being fun to try, others provide lots of benefit in both learning and practical applications.
As an example, in the past we created a d-shop coin virtual currency on Hyperledger, the idea was to reward returning equipment with credits. It was a fun group effort building and learning.

This time our plan is to leverage BTP and Build Code to help us rapidly create the new baseline for the inventory application. We are opening this project within the NsQ office to volunteers so others can learn as we build. We plan to share our steps in the community along the journey with our successes, failures, examples and perhaps seeding new ideas from feedback we get in the comments.

First steps, we will work on our MVP requirements and from that create prompts to help generate the first version of the application.

Watch this space....

entry.title: Re: Description with capital letters ignored in IBP excel
entry.content: Please be careful with mixed case. You might not see what you expect if you filter by the description with mixed case or you use it update values without other attributes that make it unique. When I filter in IBP data view, I don't get back both spellings. When I display keyfigure, with just the mixed case attribute and no filters, I get two rows with values.

entry.title: Default Line Items on Object Page SAP RAP
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

 

I am working on an SAP RAP project and developing a multi-line edit app. The app consists of a parent entity, where the "Version" field serves as the key. This parent entity is displayed on the List Page. Additionally, I have a child entity that uses version and one other field as key. This child entity is displayed on the Object Page.

 

My Requirement is to display three default line items on the object page after the user clicks the Create button on the List Page. 

Any advice or suggestions on how to implement this would be greatly appreciated.

 

Thank You!

entry.title: Re: Is standard field Employee Profile "country" syncroni...
entry.content:
Hi @dsarzisartori,

The HRIS sync mapping for the Country is hard-coded in the system using the Coprorate Address of the Location (Job Information > Location > Corporate Address > Country).

Another option is to synchronize the "Country of Company" (which is derived from the Country of Registration value defined on the Legal Entity) to the Country field in the Platform, but this need to be configured.

Please check this KBA for more details. 2618726 - Syncing the Country of Company between Employee Central and the SuccessFactors Platform.

If this information helped you, please, Accept as Solution.

Cristian Ocampo

entry.title: Re: Batch reporting with Excel to CR 2020
entry.content:
You can write your own application for doing this (not trivial).

An easier solution is to use a 3rd-party Crystal Report automation tools. Ken Hamady maintain a list of such tools here. Several of the tools on that list can handle your use case.

entry.title: Re: Build Process Automation Notifications Part 1 of 3 - Default Notifications
entry.content:
Thanks for the heads-up. What is "SAP-standard Alert Notification Service" you refer to in here? Where can I find documentation for this? This name is very similar to SAP Alert Notification service for SAP BTP, but I guess you are not referring to this. From the description in the document you point to, this appears to be Notifications in Work Zone. This looks vastly different from the service listed in discovery center. 

What you mention here is notifications about tasks assigned to a person. Typically in a workflow, some participants need to be informed; the workflow does not have to wait for them to undertake any tasks. Is there a plan to provide support for these notifications in Process Automation? Today, an email task is used for this. It would be better to get (choice of channel for example) what you mention here with the possibility to see the status of the task in Work Zone without a custom implementation.

entry.title: Re: local setup commece cloud 2211 build failure groovyc
entry.content: The problem may be related to the gradle version used. The platform requires version 7.3.3 for the build. If you installed 8.x on your machine or use and IDE such as IntelliJ, which uses gradle 8.x by default, you will get errors like that.

entry.title: Re: BW/4HANA Cockpit - process-chain-monitor no longer sh...
entry.content: ODQ_TQ_JOB has no direct relation to ODP. It is the worker job of the ODQ Task Framework (Daemon CL_ODQ_QUEUED_TASK_DAEMON). The ODP framework uses this framework to trigger various things like the ODQ_CLEANUP and the ODQ_DAEMON jobs. ODQ Task Framework and ODP are not the same.

entry.title: Easing the transition: 7 steps for a business data fabric deployment with SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
Driving change, especially in a business context, is a process that takes time and effort. Success hinges on robust collaboration, meticulous planning, and the application of appropriate technology solutions.

Likewise, transforming your company's data landscape demands coordination and planning among your business and IT teams. A business data fabric architecture is essential. This architecture focuses on delivering an integrated, semantically rich data layer over underlying data landscapes, to provide seamless and scalable access to data without duplication.

SAP Datasphere is the foundation for a business data fabric.

In previous blogs, I’ve discussed the advantages and ROI of building a business data fabric with SAP Datasphere. In this blog, I will share seven steps for implementing a business data fabric, designed to help you follow best practices and achieve a smoother, faster build:

Step 1: Review your current data architecture

Audit your current processes to build a complete map of your data sources, systems, and flows, to identify any gaps and challenges you need to address.

Step 2: Define your data governance framework

Determine the comprehensive set of policies, processes, and standards that will be implemented across your company to ensure the accuracy, consistency, and security of all your data.

Step 3: Design your business data fabric architecture

Create a single, unified data model that incorporates the data sources, data governance policies, and security protocols you’ve established.

Step 4: Integrate your data into a single view

Using your newly defined model, connect your data sources both inside and outside the organization and integrate them across systems and user applications.

Step 5: Deploy your data governance policies

Implement the governance and security protocols you have established, ensuring that data quality, lineage, and masking rules are thoroughly verified before granting data access to users. This will help in maintaining the integrity and security of your data.

Step 6: Configure AI and analytics

Use analytics and automation to actively harness the potential of your business data fabric in real time, leveraging AI to deliver actionable and in-context insights to all relevant stakeholders.

Step 7: Communicate and manage the changes

Implement the cultural changes that a business data fabric architecture requires, ensuring that your company is prepared to adopt new data management practices and promote the use of data across different teams and business areas.

Take the next step

Ready to build your own business data fabric with SAP Datasphere? Get started by visiting our business data fabric page.

entry.title: Data Templates for Concur Data Delivery Service (DDS): SAP Mission
entry.content:
Overview

The Data Templates for the Concur Data Delivery Service (DDS) Expense content package offers a powerful solution for visualizing and analyzing data captured in SAP Concur Expense. Seamlessly integrated with SAP Data Delivery Services and utilizing the SAP Analytics Cloud for data visualization, this solution provides administrators with comprehensive insights into organizational expenses, system administration, and user distributions. Its advanced analytics capabilities include robust auditing and control features essential for fraud detection and compliance assurance.

Built upon Analytical Models in SAP Datasphere and visualized in SAP Analytics Cloud, this content package encompasses a suite of detailed stories that deliver in-depth perspectives on expense management. These insights empower administrators to make informed decisions, optimize expense workflows, and enhance overall financial governance.

SAP MISSIONS

What are Missions? Missions include best practices and step-by-step guidance for implementing business use cases, with a well-established support from the community and mission experts, for free of cost. Missions also guide your project team throughout the adoption phases of SAP Business Technology Platform. SAP Missions are created by SAP Product Teams to help you address your pain-points and solve business problems. These missions span across the complete adoption lifecycle with the goal of go live. Missions include best practices and step-by-step guidance for implementing use cases, with a well-established support from mission experts and SAP Community.

Extract your Concur Data Delivery Service using SAP Integration Suite Mission

Use Case 

If you are using the Data Delivery Service from Concur and have subscribed to the necessary SAP BTP services listed in the prerequisites below, you can take advantage of the pre-built content available across these services through Business Content. This approach will significantly reduce the effort required to build the data pipeline end-to-end. Instead, you will only need a few hours to import, configure, and use this content. This Discovery Mission will guide you through the process of using SAP Integration Suite to extract data from the Concur Data Delivery Service, load it into SAP Datasphere, and then deploy the SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) Business Content for Concur Analytics.

Current Position - What is the challenge?

The challenge is to bring SAP Concur spend data into SAP tools. This improves the user experience, decision-making, and financial and operational planning through relevant visualizations and frequent updates. It also sets a foundation for future use of SAC Enterprise smart features and integration with other data sources.

Destination - What is the outcome?

- Improve decision-making and financial/operational planning by presenting customers with a more comprehensive understanding of their Concur expenditure data, as SAC narratives provide a more interconnected view compared to Intelligence reports.
- Enhance the precision and completeness of data provided to customers.
- Provide frequent prompt insights numerous times a day.
- Establish a base for future use of SAC smart features and for integration with other data sources.

How You Get There - What is the solution?

The Data Delivery Service with Data Templates provides customers with their Concur data and a prebuilt pipeline using SAP BTP services. This includes not only today's data but also historical data from up to the last two years and all current transactional data. This way, companies can find new insights, and increase workflow efficiencies and cost savings.

Next Steps

To learn more and to complete this mission - click HERE! 
 

 

* Travel, Travel Allowance, Invoice & Request data on Roadmap for future releases *

Resources:

  • https://www.concur.com/data-delivery-service
  • https://api.sap.com/package/SAPConcurExpenseDDSIntegrationwithSAPDatasphere/overview
  • https://community.sap.com/t5/spend-management-blogs-by-sap/data-templates-for-dds-expense-administra...
  • https://community.sap.com/t5/spend-management-blogs-by-sap/data-templates-for-concur-data-delivery-e...
  • https://community.sap.com/t5/spend-management-blogs-by-sap/data-templates-for-concur-data-delivery-s...
  • https://community.sap.com/t5/spend-management-blogs-by-sap/data-templates-for-concur-data-delivery-s...
  • https://community.sap.com/t5/spend-management-blogs-by-sap/data-templates-for-concur-data-delivery-service-dds-risk-amp-compliance-sac/ba-p/13861548
SAP Concur SAP Integration Suite SAP Datasphere SAP Analytics Cloud

- Item4: 20 qty

Shafi

entry.title: Modify Form Route Map pertaining to PMGM
entry.content:
Hey Peeps!

I have a query pertaining to Modify Form Route Map.

Requirement: Modifying the Route map due dates after the forms are live and completed the Mid Year steps.
Enabling the form to move to the next steps based on the new schedule.

Process Followed: As mentioned above the forms needs be routed on the new scheduled route map dates. 
Used the "Modify Form Route Map" transaction to update the dates and checked the "Automatic Send on Due Date" checkbox using the KBA 2087312.

Initial Configuration: Initially in the route map the due dates were set for October however, the dates needs to be set for future. Automatic Send on Due date checkbox wasn't enabled.

Issue: The dates in the forms are getting updated, however, the forms are not getting routed as per the new schedule.

Please help me if there is any workaroud to route the forms as per the new schedule OR justify the issue.

SAP SuccessFactors Performance & Goals #

entry.title: SAP Build Code at Devtoberfest by SAP TechEd 2024
entry.content:
Hey everyone!

Devtoberfest is almost here - and it's Devtoberfest by SAP TechEd in 2024! 🍻

This year, Devtoberfest, the celebration of SAP technologies, is getting closer to SAP's premiere tech conference, SAP TechEd. The free, virtual event will run from September 23 to October 25, 2024, with a break during the week of SAP TechEd Virtual 2024. All sessions will be livestreamed/premiered on the SAP Developers' channel on YouTube.  

 

You can find a list of all the session content in the SAP Community events area: Devtoberfest Events.

  • Monday: 🟢 ABAP & CAP
  • Tuesday: 🟣 Tooling (for SAP Build Apps and SAP Build Code)
  • Wednesday: 🔴 Integration
  • Thursday: 🔵 Machine Learning, AI, and Data
  • Friday: 🟠 Frontend

 

Below is a detailed overview of the SAP Build Code sessions, including abstracts, schedules, speakers, and links. 
ℹ️ Click on the session titles to navigate to the respective event pages in the dedicated SAP Community Group, where you can already start communicating with the speakers, your peers, and the event organizers. Indicate your interest in the session in the "Will you be attending?" and use the "Add to Calendar" option to save the date and time to your individual calendar.
🔔 You can also use the "Notify me" feature on YouTube to be notified when livestreams and premieres start!

Enjoy the event! 🍻

 

 

🟣 Develop a Full-Stack CAP Application with SAP Build Code

Learn how to generate a complete, full-stack CAP application, including sample data, with a single prompt using Joule in SAP Build Code. Enhance the application with additional functionality using custom handlers and CAP plugins.

Speaker: Daniel Schlachter (SAP)
📅 Sept 24, 2024 | ⏰ 11:00 A.M - 12:PM AM CEST

Add to Calender

 

 

🟣 Cross-Platform Mobile App Development with SAP Build Code and the MDK

Learn how to create and enhance cross-platform enterprise mobile applications for both iOS and Android using Mobile Development Kit (MDK). This session covers getting started by creating your first app from the SAP Build Lobby and enhancing it in the Build Code development environment. You will learn how to leverage a single code base to deliver robust and visually appealing applications. The session will also introduce new debugging tools to monitor logs and network traces. By the end of the session, you will be equipped with the skills to build, deploy, and maintain scalable and efficient mobile applications and to tailor them to your enterprise's unique needs using SAP Build Code.

Speaker: Bill Froelich (SAP)
📅 Oct 1, 2024 | ⏰ 6:00 P.M CEST- 7:00 PM CEST

Add to Calender

 

 

🟠 Build Elegant Enterprise Apps for Android with SAP Fiori and SAP Build Code

Learn to design and create enterprise mobile applications for the Android operating system. This session begins with an idea for an app and data, demonstrating how to apply SAP Fiori for Android Design Guidelines to design an effective and visually appealing user interface. Using Jetpack Compose and SAP BTP SDK for Android, we will guide you through the development process, ensuring your app runs smoothly across various Android devices and form factors. By the end of the session, you'll have a clear understanding of how to design and build robust, scalable, and functional Android applications tailored to your enterprise needs.

Speaker: Natalie Stainton (SAP) , Mark Fogle (SAP)
📅 Oct 4, 2024 | ⏰ 5:30 P.M CEST- 6:30 PM CEST

Add to Calender

 

 

🟠 Craft Enterprise Apps for iOS, iPadOS, and visionOS with SAP Fiori and SAP Build Code

Explore how to design and develop enterprise mobile applications for Apple platforms, including iOS, iPadOS, and visionOS. This session begins with an idea for an app and data, demonstrating how to apply SAP Fiori for iOS Design Guidelines to create an intuitive and visually compelling user interface. Leveraging SwiftUI and SAP BTP SDK for iOS, we will demonstrate the development process, ensuring your app delivers a seamless experience across various devices. By the end of the session, you will be equipped with the knowledge to build sophisticated, scalable, and efficient applications tailored to your enterprise's unique needs.

Speaker: Huy Ngo (SAP) , Sheng Xu (SAP)
📅 Oct 4, 2024 | ⏰ 7:00 P.M CEST- 8:00 PM CEST

Add to Calender

 

 

Have I aroused your curiosity? Then attend the various sessions to learn more about the latest and greatest SAP Build Code innovations and best practices.

You can also check out the SAP Build Code Sessions at SAP TechEd in 2024

We look forward to interacting with you!

entry.title: SAP Build Code Sessions at SAP TechEd in 2024
entry.content:
Hello Everyone !

That time of year is here again, SAP TechEd 2024 is just around the corner! 

 

SAP TechEd is the leading tech conference for all developers, IT professionals, business experts, tech visionaries, innovators, and leaders eager to gain in-depth knowledge of SAP’s solutions, platforms, and technologies, along with insights into their future direction. It also offer an opportunity to network and connect with peers and SAP experts. 

SAP TechEd in 2024 will kick off with a free, engaging virtual event accessible to everyone, followed by SAP TechEd on Tour stops all around the world for a unique opportunity for more in-depth, in-person learning experiences. The series of local on-site events will be organized in partnership with and hosted by SAP User Groups and SAPinsider. Learn more about the virtual experience here.

The choice is yours: Join us virtually for the first event in the series, SAP TechEd Virtual 2024! 

📌Save the date and register now!
📅 October 8 - 9, 2024

At this year's SAP TechEd, you will have the opportunity to attend a variety of sessions and formats - such as lectures, jumpstarts, and roadmaps - organized into six (6) content tracks that are aligned with your business needs, covering SAP technologies, products, and solutions:

  • Digital Transformation with Cloud ERP (DT)
  • SAP Business Technology Platform – General (XP)
  • Application Development and Automation (AD)
  • Data and Analytics (DA)
  • Integration (IN)
  • Artificial Intelligence (AI)

 

📚Access the full session catalog of SAP TechEd Virtual 2024

 

SAP Build Code - Sessions

 

📅Tuesday, Oct 8

 

1. AD100 | Unlock SAP Build and ABAP Cloud interoperability to elevate your extensions

[Track Overview, 45 min] Discover a new approach to building extensions while keeping your core clean. Learn how SAP Build solutions and the ABAP Cloud development model work together to foster fusion development. Whether you prefer pro-code or low-code tools, you can smoothly integrate them to build extensions tailored to your needs. Learn from experts about best practices, innovative solutions, and practical applications that can enhance your development processes and drive business growth.
Speakers: Dr. Alexander Rother (SAP) , Bjorn Schulz (SAP) , Andre Borchert (SAP)
Schedule: 📅 Tuesday, Oct 8 | 🕛 6:00 PM – 6:45 PM CEST​

 

2. AD105 | Enhance your fusion team collaboration with ABAP Cloud and SAP Build

[Lecture, 25 min] Learn how to take advantage of interoperability between SAP’s low-code and pro-code solutions. Discover how you can use the graphical modeler in the development environment of SAP Build Code for the ABAP Cloud development model and how your ABAP Cloud projects can be integrated into the lobby of SAP Build solutions.
Speakers: Tim Back (SAP) , Anne Keller (SAP) 
Schedule: 📅 Tuesday, Oct 8 | 🕛 7:00 PM – 7:25 PM CEST​

 

📅Wednesday, Oct 9

 

3. AD104 | SAP Build Code: AI development and new service integrations with CAP

[Lecture, 25 min] Examine new capabilities of SAP Cloud Application Programming Model (CAP) in SAP Build Code, and experience how the Joule copilot can speed up your extension development. In addition, find out how service integrations with CAP can reduce development effort, help you avoid pitfalls, and allow for evolution without disruption following the clean core strategy.
Speakers: David Kunz (SAP) , Daniel Schlachter (SAP) 
Schedule: 📅 Wednesday, Oct 9 | 🕛 11:30 AM – 11:55 AM CEST​

 

4. AD101 | Tailor your UI with SAPUI5 in SAP Build Code

[Lecture, 25 min] Discover the latest on SAPUI5 development in SAP Build Code, providing a modern user experience across ABAP and SAP Cloud Application Programming Model. Learn how to realize clean core extensibility with new capabilities and how you can future-proof your app with new tools and best practices. Understand why SAP Web Components are strategic and how you can benefit from using them.
Speakers: Oliver Graeff (SAP) , Peter Muessig (SAP) 
Schedule: 📅 Wednesday, Oct 9 | 🕛 12:00 PM – 12:25 PM CEST​

 

5. AD103 | Extend your SAP solutions with custom mobile apps using SAP Build Code

[Lecture, 25 min] Leverage our mobile development kit, SAP BTP SDK for iOS, and SAP BTP SDK for Android to create extensible enterprise-grade mobile apps. Learn how you can transform your user experience with SAP Mobile Services and AI capabilities in SAP Build Code.

Speakers: Sami Lechner (SAP) , Sandeep Tetali (SAP) 
Schedule: 📅 Wednesday, Oct 9 | 🕛 12:30 PM – 12:55 PM CEST​

 

📅Thursday, Oct 10

 

6. AD181 | Develop applications and processes with SAP Build and Joule

[Jump-Start, 25 min] Immerse yourself in creating comprehensive intelligent applications using SAP Build solutions in this hands-on session. Gain comprehensive knowledge of full-stack development, process automation, and business site creation with SAP Build. Learn how SAP Cloud Application Programming Model is leveraged in a highly productive development environment with Joule technology.

Speakers: Eric Solberg (SAP) , Patrick Schad (SAP) 
Schedule: 📅 Thursday, Oct 10 | 🕛 9:00 AM – 9:25 AM CEST​

 

 

I hope this overview gives you a few more reasons to join us at SAP TechEd 2024.

We look forward to meeting and interacting with you virtually at SAP TechEd Virtual and in person at the local events in West Palm Beach (US), Melbourne (AUS), Copenhagen (DK), Birmingham (UK), and Wiesbaden (DE).

In the meantime, you can check out this overview of the SAP Build Code Session at Devtoberfest which will take place virtually from Sept 23 to Oct 25, 2024.

Stay tuned - and see you there!

entry.title: Re: Launching worksheet by employee group
entry.content:
Hi there,

We always create worksheets by Line Managers within a specific scope using the worksheets launch groups you are referring to or like @kemartinez is mentionning using the Compensation hierarchy.

As far as I am concerned I prefer avoiding using them when I can because it takes so much longer for the system to create the worksheets and also makes troubleshooting of which employee belongs in which worksheet a lot more complex)

That being said we usually grant access to HR to the salary review cycle through the Executive Review feature (controlled by RBP) and not as a part of the approval steps.

All the best,

Xavier

entry.title: Re: SAP MDK - How to access object information from a Car...
entry.content:
Yes, I have confirmed there appears to be a bug related to the context / binding on the Card Collection.  You can work around it using a rule to set the action binding based on the selected card in the short term and I will open an internal issue to work on a solution.

Here is an example rule that gets the card binding and calls setActionBinding() to correctly set the default binding object before navigating to the next page.

/**
 * Describe this function...
 * @Param {IClientAPI} context
 */
export default function CardToDetail(context) {
    let selectedCard = context.getParent().binding;
    context.getPageProxy().setActionBinding(selectedCard);
    context.getPageProxy().executeAction({
        "Name": '/SCN_00033/Actions/GenericNavigation.action',
        "Properties": {
            "PageToOpen": "/SCN_00033/Pages/SampleServiceV4_SalesOrderItems/SalesOrderItems_Detail.page"
        }
    });
}

entry.title: Integrating IPS with SuccessFactors: Handling Project-Specific Scenarios
entry.content:
Introduction

Hare Krishna !

Integrating Identity Provisioning Services (IPS) with SAP SuccessFactors(SF) enhances identity management however involves key scenarios like password migration for existing users, automating initial password creation using different logics, creation of Password by reading from a Password field from SF or using combinations of these to meet client requirements. This blog explores these challenges, providing insights and solutions to ensure a smooth, secure integration process.

 

Consideration:

Source System: SAP Success Factors Application

Target System: SAP Identity authentication service

 

Concepts:

1)How attributes are read from Success factors and Written in IAS using IPS source system and Target system transformation:

2)Reading custom fields from Successfactors

There are only few standard field from SAP Success factors which gets synced by IPS standards transformation code. Check Manage Workforce SCIM API in SuccessFactors for more details. You can select and unselect existing attributes but maintain a new attribute here.


If required additional fields can also be read from SuccessFactors using custom Attributes (15 custom attributes are available) which can be used for different purpose like creation of filters, writing it to custom attributes in Target or in creation of new password.

You will need to perform below activities

  • Perform mapping of SAP SF standard fields with custom attributes in Manage business configurations

 

  • Update transformation code in Source System to Read the values and Target System to perform the require action using those attribute values.

For example - in above screenshot we are reading date of birth from SF (which is mapped to SF custom05).

Add below code in Source system to read it. Value in Target Path can be used in Target transformation (as sourcePath) to perform required action.

 

{
                "sourcePath": "$['urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:extension:successfactors:2.0:User']['customFields'][?(@.customFieldName == 'custom05')]['value']",
                "targetPath": "$.custom05",
                "optional": true
            },

 

Target Transformation - To write the date of birth value to custom attribute 1 in IAS for users which are getting synced.

 

{
                "sourcePath": "$.custom05",
                "targetPath": "$['urn:sap:cloud:scim:schemas:extension:custom:2.0:User']['attributes'][0]['value']",
                "optional": true
            },
            {
                "targetPath": "$['urn:sap:cloud:scim:schemas:extension:custom:2.0:User']['attributes'][0]['name']",
                "condition": "$.custom05" EMPTY false",
                "constant": "customAttribute1"
            },

 

3)IPS don't perform the Password migration.

  • After IAS activation is performed by SAP SF, when user perform the login for the first time, Password is migrated from SF to IAS using the configuration performed in Authentication providers.

Configuration required for password configuration is in Authentication Providers

  • IPS transformation code which is required to updated for users so that connectivity works as expected.

 

 {
                "targetPath": "$.sourceSystemId",
                "constant": "<CompanyID>"
            },
            {
                "targetPath": "$.sourceSystem",
                "constant": "100"
            },
            {
                "targetPath": "$.sendMail",
                "constant": "false",
                "scope": "createEntity"
            },
            {
                "targetPath": "$.passwordStatus",
                "constant": "enable",
                "scope": "createEntity"
            },

 

4) While password creation for users in IAS, how to make sure that user changes password on first login.

  • Transformation code - Make sure PasswordStatus Constant is "initial", this will ensure that It prompt user to change the password on first login.

 

{
                "targetPath": "$.passwordStatus",
                "constant": "initial",
                "scope": "createEntity"
            },

 

Now let's discuss different scenarios requested by Clients in multiple projects:

Scenario 1

Project have SAP SF instance and client is performing the upgrade to IAS. Client requires existing users to login with same passwords.

Solution

Follow the steps mentioned in Concept 3 and configure - Authentication provider to perform the password migration for users when they login for the first time. Make sure to update the transformation code so that it don't trigger onboarding emails and update users in IAS with Company ID and Source system details so that it can connect to Success Factors instance at the time of password migration (first login post IAS upgrade).

 

Scenario 2

Project have new SAP SF instance which is already integrated with IAS. Client wants to configure a mechanism to create default password for new user using logic (for example - username +"HK!"). Once user performs first login, It ask to create a new password for user.

Solution:

As username is already getting fetched as standard attribute, else if any other attribute is required (use concept 2 - Source system transformation to fetch it). No update needed in Source system transformation.

Target Transformation:

This will ensure the logic of password creation

 

 {
                "targetPath": "$.password",
                "scope": "createEntity"
                "sourcePath": "$.userName",
                "optional": true,
                "functions": [
                    {
                        "function": "concatString",
                        "suffix": "HK!"
                    }

 

This will ensure user is prompted to change the password on first login:

 

{
                "targetPath": "$.passwordStatus",
                "constant": "initial",
                "scope": "createEntity"
            },

 

 

Scenario 3

Project have new SAP SF instance which is already integrated with IAS.

  1. Client have created a field in SF called SF initial password field.
  2. Onboarding team will create this password in this field and requirement is to create user in IAS with password getting fetched from this field.
  3. Once user performs first login, It should ask to create new password for user.

Solution:

Follow concept 2 to perform the mapping of custom password field and fetch it from success factors. Update Target Transformation code as below:

 

{
                "sourcePath": "$.custom05",
                "targetPath": "$.password",
                "scope": "createEntity"
            }

 

Follow steps mentioned in concept 4 to make sure it prompt to create new password for users on first login.

 

Scenario 4

This is a mixed requirement where Project have an already existing SAP SF instance. Once integration with IAS is performed using upgrade center, Client wants existing user should be able to login with their existing passwords. For New users, client wants to read the password from SAP SF field. No users should loose the access.

Solution

Perform the steps mentioned in Concept 3 to make sure existing users perform password migration on the first login. Perform the full sync after updating the transformation code.

Now pause the sync job. Update the Transformation logic so that it will now fetch the data from SF and create new password for new users and ask to change it on first loginIn. (Refer Scenario 3 for transformation code).

Run the sync job again - configure it run after some interval so that new user onboarding happens properly.

now Old users which were already there in SF will use SF password for first time and for new users getting onboarded , it will create new password.

Scenario 5

Project have IP restriction enabled. This enables them to login to SAP SF only in Client VPN.

  • Now client wants to remove this and enable 2 factor authentication for external vendors who login to SF using password. Enable Password creation for only these users by reading it from SF field.
  • Use SAP SF field - loginMethod to filter the users with login Method field value as PWD in a PWDGroup in IAS,.
  • Use risk based authentication to enable 2 factor authentication only for PWDGroup.

Solution

It contains multiple steps.

  • Create Groups in IAS PWD_Group and SSO_Group and update target transformation logic to sync users to SSO and PWD group according to the value in LoginMethod field. If it's blank - it's considered as SSO. Also put the condition in password creation logic to only create it for users with LoginMethod field as PWD

 

{
                "sourcePaths": [
                    "$.loginMethod"
                ],
                "targetPath": "$.groups[0].value",
                "valueMappings": [
                    {
                        "key": [
                            "PWD"
                        ],
                        "mappedValue": "PWD_Group"
                    },
                    {
                        "key": [
                            "SSO"
                        ],
                        "mappedValue": "SSO_Group"
                    },
                    {
                        "key": [
                            ""
                        ],
                        "mappedValue": "SSO_Group"
                    }
                ],
                "type": "valueMapping",
                "defaultValue": "SSO_Group"
            },


 {
                "sourcePath": "$.custom05",
                "targetPath": "$.password",
                "condition": "$.loginMethod == 'PWD'",
                "optional": true,
                "scope": "createEntity"
            }

 

  • Update the Risk based authentication in SF Application in IAS to enable 2 factor authentication for PWD group users.

  • Disable the IP restriction in Successfactors

SAP Reference documents:

  • Integrating SuccessFactors with Identity Authentication IAS through the Upgrade Center
  • How to Use SCIM customFields from SuccessFactors
  • Configure HRIS Sync Mappings in Employee Central using Manage Business Configuration
  • System: How to restrict access to SuccessFactors by IP address - IP Restriction Management
  •  How to use loginMethod for users to login to SAP SuccessFactors with SSO and PWD once the Upgrade run

Conclusion

This blog highlights key strategies for integrating Identity Provisioning Services (IPS) with SAP SuccessFactors (SF) and SAP Identity Authentication Service (IAS). It covers effective password migration, custom attribute handling, and automated password creation, ensuring a secure and efficient transition while addressing diverse client needs.

entry.title: Re: Schedule a job for SAP integration suite iflow from S...
entry.content:
We can deploy an iflow on production environment with start timer option. But after some days if business wants to change the execution time then it will have additional development efforts. Again, the integration developer will have to do the changes in start timer and again deploy the iflow in production. We want to avoid that.

We want to handle/control the iflow execution without start timer.

I have configured the endpoints for iflow and I want to know the steps to trigger an iflow endpoint from job scheduler service.

Thanks 🙂

entry.title: Contract with completion rule does not allow more than one release sales order
entry.content:
Hello SD Masters,

I faced I scenario by which customer needs to have a balance control between volume contract and referenced sales orders.

In resume:

Contract was created with 12 tons. Customer asked us to make possible to have the split of these quantity in 3 sales orders of 4 tons.

The item category of contract is assigned completion rule = "C- Item is completed after the target qty is fully referenced".

Copy control is settled with "+", in field Pos./Neg. Quan.

After creation of 1st. sales order with 4 tons. there is a message error at the momento of creation of 2nd. sales order:

"The reference has already been completely copied or rejected

Message no. V2015

Diagnosis

The reference document cannot be further referenced because all items have either been referenced or rejected"

I had an idea that the system would have a balance control, between contract and sales order. That is, this message would be shown only after I consumed the 12 tons. from the contract.

Is this a standard behavior from S/4, or something can be wrong, for here?

Best regards, and thanks!!!

entry.title: Re: Send the PR data to HANA without Budget check
entry.content:
Solution is 

Set the parameter Application.Budget.CheckBudgetDuringFinalApproval to disabled and the approvers of PR should not belong to Budget.approver group.

entry.title: Re: We need to overlap multiple Sales Order Blanket Agree...
entry.content:
You can do that by enabling the option in General Settings / BP / Blanket Agreements

* Allow Multiple Blanket Agreements for Same Period.

entry.title: Re: New Pipeline Concept features: new partner id definition, alternative partner, bypass option

entry.title: Re: Send the PR data to HANA without Budget check
entry.content:
Please also check this whether it is working

 

To skip a budget check, the parameter Application.Budget.ReqSubmitOnError needs to be enabled and the below-mentioned steps have to be followed:

  1. Edit the requisition.
  2. Access the Budget Details link under Cart Summary.
  3. Select Action > Skip Budget located in the lower right corner.
  4. Click Save.

Now, you can approve the requisition. These actions only skip the budget check for that particular requisition; it does not affect your site's budget code allocation. You must act as or be a member of the Budget Approver group to take these actions.
